title: Vacuum Cycle

[[File:1130-2.gif|2000px]]
{{Image Banner|style=float: center;|image=LEVEL698.png}} {{Audio|file=698thm.mp3|volume=0.5}} [[File:698lantern.png|center|300px]] Look into the lanterns flame. Listen to the sounds, the way it burns. What do you see? Does it speak to you? Every flame tells its own [[Ineterna|unique]] [[Ineterna/TRINITY|story]]. They represent the inevitable cycle of passion, destruction, and transformation. Of course, just like any old book, a fire can leave a burning impression. To [[IDI|some]], that may be temporary, [[The Deal|a mere inconvenience]] to them before it becomes laughable. While [[The Umbra|others]], may carry that transformation their entire life... But how long? Or more importantly, how.. does it happen? A passion, once it is fulfilled, it is destroyed. Tossed, left out, and forgotten. But when the catalyst strikes, the transformation begins. The object undergoes metamorphosis, resulting in it becoming something unrecognizable. This will happen, again, and again, and again. And finally, when it has run out of fuel, when it no longer has a use. It fades, smoldering away, until not even a spark remains. Do you understand what I mean? Look closer. The lanterns you see above the paths, they remember. Each one carries the story of a flame long gone, a memory reborn into light. They drift here, between endings and beginnings, suspended in this place where even gravity itself has forgotten what it was. When they flicker, it is not the wind that stirs them. It is remembrance. The echoes of passions once fulfilled, of purposes once extinguished. You can listen, if you’re quiet enough. The burning you hear is not fire, but the sound of something trying to regain its humanity again. Its will of life, of existing. '''For every flame, once it dies, seeks another form...''' {{Collapse|num=1|initial='''''That is the cycle...'''''|final='''''That is the cycle...'''''|body=
[[File:698bg.png|2000px]]
» VACUUM CYCLE «
{{Blurimage|caption=Interconnected.|file=698bg.png|align=center}} "Vacuum Cycle" is an anomalous location thought to reside on the boundary between '''The Backrooms''' and '''The Void'''. To the naked eye, it appears as an endless interconnected network of deep violet pathways, drifting in a starless expanse. Each path carries a faint glow, its surface resembling smooth purple concrete, with semi-transparent strings of dim light. These structures lack any visible foundation; instead, they float motionless in the abyss, turning, twisting, and intersecting in ways that seem impossible. Existing as the absolute boundary between existence and absence, '''Vacuum Cycle''' represents the brink before complete [[Nullification]]. The paths in the level are thought to be massive [[Object 300|Subvoid Fragments]], colossal remnants that have fused together into a giant, ring-like construct hypothesized to surround '''The Backrooms'''. Their immensely close proximity to '''The Void''' has rendered the level in a paradoxical state of partially unstable, yet self-sustaining. Beyond the boundaries of the level lies '''The Void''', where direct exposure results in immediate '''Nullification'''. As such, '''Vacuum Cycle''' is regarded by the '''V.C.U.''' as the closet frontier to '''The Void''' ever documented and observed. Gravity within this location behaves otherworldly, as it seemingly adjusts its orientation below the wanderers feet. This allows wanderers to traverse twisting slopes, vertical loops, or even inverted pathways without realizing it. As a result, visual distortion is fairly common when wandering the level. Distant paths may appear closer than they actually are, and vice versa. Curiously, the level seems to reject the idea of true descent; those who step off the edges of a path do not plummet endlessly into '''The Void'''. Rather, they drift soundlessly before landing on another segment of the network, with their gravity orientation resetting to match that of their initial entry point. Scattered throughout the pathways are floating lanterns, drifting weightlessly above the surfaces and emitting a soft, violet glow. Their flames seem to fade between hues of pink and indigo, which cast shifting reflections on the pathways below. When the lanterns sway, they produce a faint melodic jingling sound, echoing through the vast emptiness. [[File:698door.png|center|400px]] On occasion, wanderers may encounter a mysterious lone door, its surface containing the same violet-blue color as the pathway it stands on. The door, unsupported by walls yet standing upright, appears to lead nowhere from the outside. However, opening it reveals a short passage leading to a spiral staircase, its steps and central pillar sharing the same hue as the paths outside. Reaching the top of the stairwell leads to a small circular room, the ceiling arching overhead reminiscent of a small observatory. {{Blurimage|caption=Shining.|file=ASTRAL698OBJECT.png|align=center}} Looking outside the window present in the room reveals '''Astromida''', a colossal celestial body appearing as a planetary-star hybrid. The star-like planet is adjourned with colorful glowing rings that shimmer softly in hues of purple. Though it feels both distant and near, it casts a soft glow in the room. It is believed '''Astromida''' formed during the early creation of '''Vacuum Cycle'''. Over time, the '''Subvoid Fragments''' that failed to merge with the main level fused together under immense pressure, creating a self-sustaining mass of heat and energy that now resides in the level as one of the most mysterious objects in '''The Backrooms'''. [[File:698exitandenter.png|center|500px]] The most common way to enter this level involves finding a mysterious violet-hued door, which tends to appear in regions where '''Nullification''' rates are more high. Upon passing through, the door will silently close and vanish from both sides, leaving no trace of its existence. In rarer cases, there exists an almost impossible chance that an individual falling into '''The Void''', or becoming trapped within an area undergoing '''Nullification''', will instead awaken on one of the violet pathways. One way to exit is to retrace the path to where one first arrived. After an unknown duration, the violet door will reappear, allowing return to the previous location or level. However, on rare occasions, wanderers reported finding a mysterious patch of grass in a platform among the violet pathways, clearly out of place. No-clipping through this patch will transport the individual to '''Level 860'''.
'''Recovered Log — [V.C.U. Audio Transcript #698-01]''' '''Date:''' September 14th, 2021 '''Recovered From:''' Base Equilibrium, Post-Return Debrief ---- '''Mark''': Hey Caesar, guess who's back? '''Caesar''': Huh—what the... Mark?! I thought—how are you even here?! '''Mark''': Well, after a year in '''The Backrooms''', you kind of get used to being thrown around to random places. I just... eventually found my way back. '''Caesar:''' But you went ''into The Void''! We thought you were gone for good! '''Mark:''' I nearly was. But... thanks to a certain ''possible'' level, I made it out alive. '''Caesar:''' What do you mean, “possible”? What did you find out there? '''Mark:''' I don’t know what to call it. It was... quiet. Empty. But not like The Void. There were these long, glowing purple paths, floating in nothing. '''Caesar:''' You’re saying there’s a ''level'' right near The Void? That’s impossible. '''Mark:''' I thought so too. But while I was wandering, I kept feeling weird, like I wasn't supposed to be there. '''Caesar:''' Well.. did you see anyone? Any structures? '''Mark:''' Nope. All I saw were lanterns, hundreds of them as far as I could see. They were just.. floating above the paths, burning without smoke and providing a soft jingling sound. If I'm being honest, that was kind of soothing if not eerie. '''Caesar:''' You sure all of this isn't just hallucination caused by your proximity to '''The Void'''? '''Mark:''' Maybe, but have I ever lied to you? And.. I'm still alive. '''Caesar:''' Alright, we'll log it. log it. “Witness report of possible level between The Backrooms and The Void.” Designation pending. '''Mark:''' Call it whatever you want honestly. The higher ups are probably gonna think it's just some made up myth. Which to be honest, I'd understand. I don't even think I was supposed to be there in the first place... ---Log End---
'''V.C.U. Expedition Log — “Operation Frontier”''' '''Date:''' December 17th, 2024 '''Participants:''' Thatcher, Tallic, V.C.U. Expedition Unit-7 A recovered transmission archive confirming existence of suspected “Vacuum Cycle” referenced in Log-2021-███. ---- '''...We just got a visual photo. Purple pathways, just like the old transcript said. I don’t believe this... Mark wasn’t hallucinating.''' ''It's.. it's real. The readings confirm it. It matches several aspects of '''Subvoid Fragments''', but they seemed to have merged together into.. those.'' '''So you're telling me this thing is real? After months, no.. years of brushing this off as a myth, you are saying Level 698 ''actually'' exists?''' ''If this data holds, yes. But this shouldn't even be possible. I mean, that proximity to '''The Void'''? This image shouldn't even be possible..'' '''That's the closest I've ever seen ''anything'' to The Void without it disintegrating. Damn ''Tallic'', do you realize what this means? This level could potentially be the boundary between The Backrooms and The Void!''' (Tallic is about to speak up, but is cut off by a faint jingling sound, resembling distant chimes.) ''Wait.. did you just hear that?'' '''Just like the transcript said.. those lanterns.''' (A few minutes of silence, then static is heard again.) ''Hold up, we're getting another image. Wait.. what the hell is..'' '''No way. Is that a fucking ''planet'', in a place like that?!''' ''This can't be possible. There's no way an environment so close to '''The Void''' can support a celestial body that big. Unless, that thing is also a cluster of '''Subvoid Fragments'''.'' '''Tallic, they are saying the thing is emitting heat and light. My god, so not only do we now have proof the level exists, but apparently it's housing an entire star.''' (Transmission begins to destabilize) ''Damn it, signal's breaking up.'' (Feed cuts.) ---- '''Final Note''' Recovered images and thermal readings confirm the existence of a luminous celestial body, now designated '''Astromida''', within the newly documented '''Vacuum Cycle'''.
And as the man repented for his sins of ignorance, he was given a final chance at life... {{Collapse|num=2|initial='''Then, he was cast away to be forgotten...'''|final='''''In his new forever home...'''''|body= =='''''
